subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral + independent (IMF, World Bank, US GAO/CRS, Freedom House, Lowy) + international press; state media EXCLUDED. Tiny (~18k population) and data-thin; press not separately RSF-ranked. Fully dollarized (uses the US dollar).
A 'Free' democracy (President Whipps Jr. re-elected Nov 2024), staunchly pro-US and one of Taiwan's ~12 remaining diplomatic allies, hosting the US TACMOR over-the-horizon radar.

Standout features: a Free democracy, strong fiscal recovery (debt ~56% of GDP), and the renewed ~$890m COFA, against a fat negative tail of Chinese 'grey-zone' coercion, tourism mono-dependence, and reef/climate exposure.

economic.notes: A strong fiscal recovery and low inflation, against extreme tourism mono-dependence; fully dollarized.

fiscal_state.notes: Debt fell to ~56% of GDP (FY25), a primary surplus ~2.9%, net financial worth ~80% of GDP, all CRA-23-supported. (IMF 2025 Art IV)

inflation.notes: Fell to ~0.2% (FY25) on lower commodity/utility costs - imported from the US. (IMF 2025)

banking_stability.notes: Tiny and USD-based with no central bank/lender of last resort; correspondent-banking de-risking is the structural fragility. (IMF; inference)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: Extreme tourism mono-dependence (high-end diving) externally weaponized by China, with a minimal export base. (IMF; VOA/Al Jazeera 2024)

institutional.notes: An independent judiciary and Free rating, with thin small-state capacity.

rule_of_law.notes: An independent judiciary, regular peaceful transfers, 'Free'; small-state patronage/capacity limits temper it. (Freedom House 2025)

statistical_integrity.notes: IMF Art IV produced, but data are thin/lagged, typical micro-state gaps. (IMF 2025)

civil_service_capacity.notes: Thin administrative depth and labor shortages; COFA-implementation delays expose limits. (GAO; IMF)

press_freedom.notes: Freedom House calls media independent; not separately RSF-ranked, with PRC disinformation flagged around the 2024 election. (FH 2025; IPDF)

political_social.notes: A stable democracy with a decisive 2024 re-election, eroded only at the margin by emigration.

political_stability.notes: A stable democracy with a decisive 2024 re-election and durable institutions. (FH 2025; Al Jazeera)

civil_liberties.notes: Generally upheld (CL 55/60), with minor small-society constraints. (FH 2025)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: A significant foreign-worker share (Filipino/Bangladeshi), generally orderly with limited monitoring. (inference; State Dept)

social_cohesion.notes: A cohesive, homogeneous society, with outmigration pressure the main strain. (inference)

demographics.notes: Tiny (~18k), aging, with persistent emigration to the US under COFA. (IMF; GAO)

geopolitical.notes: Deep US/Taiwan alignment, but a frontline of US-China competition facing PRC grey-zone coercion.

alliance_reliability.notes: Deep US treaty/defense alignment plus Taiwan ties; near-term dinged by US COFA-funding delays denting the 'reliable partner' credibility. (GAO; RNZ; Diplomat)

conflict_involvement.notes: Not at war, but a frontline of US-China competition - hosts US radar, faces PRC grey-zone coercion/incursions, first-island-chain exposure. (Naval News 2025; IPDF)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: Open, dollarized, and unsanctioned; de-risking is the real friction. (IMF)

physical_practical.notes: Very low crime and functional core infrastructure, against acute reef/climate exposure.

climate.notes: High islands aid resilience, but a reef-dependent economy, coral bleaching, SLR, and typhoon exposure. (WB Pacific CCDR)

healthcare.notes: Basic local care with serious cases referred off-island (Guam/Philippines/Taiwan/Hawaii). (inference; State Dept)

infrastructure.notes: A functional core but thin/aid-financed, with construction delays from COFA-fund timing. (IMF; GAO)

crime_safety.notes: Low crime and very safe, with minor transnational/drug-transit concerns. (observed)

disaster_insurance.notes: A thin commercial market against high climate/typhoon exposure, reliant on a donor/US backstop. (inference)

summary: A genuine, well-functioning democracy (FH 92/100) whose fiscal position has materially strengthened - debt cut to ~56% of GDP and a ~2.9%-of-GDP primary surplus, underpinned by the renewed ~$890m CRA-23 Compact. Its risks are concentrated and external: a tourism-mono economy that China actively weaponizes, frontline status in US-China competition (it now hosts the US TACMOR radar), and acute reef/climate exposure. US-funding delays in FY24 briefly forced trust-fund drawdowns and dented the 'reliable-partner' narrative, but alliance reliability improves over the longer horizon. The positive skew rests on institutional quality; the negative tail is geopolitical and climatic.
